bio.extra_names: Named after his mother's Griswold maiden name
bio.photo_notes: Cloudinary; supplied by Sam — c.1872-1873 portrait during mayoral term

birth.historical_notes: Per Wikipedia: b. September 4, 1830 — EH 2448 says Sept 14, 1830 (10-day discrepancy). Going with Wikipedia's primary source date.

death.note: EH says March 24, 1888; Wikipedia and Findagrave say March 6, 1888 — discrepancy. Going with EH but flagging.

burial.plot_notes: Forest Home Cemetery, Milwaukee Milwaukee Co Wisconsin

marriages.notes: Per EH 2448 + Findagrave: m1. Sept 9, 1862 Sarah Parker Harris (1837-1868), daughter of Nathaniel Harris of Middlebury VT. She d. April 27, 1868 Middlebury VT age 30 (likely childbirth complications given timing of 3 children). Three children by 1st marriage: Edward Harris (*1863-1932), Frederick Nathaniel (1865-?), William David (1867-?).

Per EH 2448 + Wikipedia + Findagrave: m2. Sept 28, 1871 Julia Almira Ashley (1839-1915), daughter of Dr. William Bliss + Julia Cornwall (Hall) Ashley of Milwaukee WI, b. Nov 16, 1839 Glastonbury CT. Per Milwaukee historical record: Julia's father was rector of St. Paul's Episcopal Church Milwaukee. Three daughters: Alice Ashley (1875-1966 m. Frank McCutcheon), Julia Elizabeth (1877-1956), Edith Mary (1885-1975 m. Greenwood).

education.notes: Per Wikipedia: graduated Middlebury College 1853. EH 2448 doesn't specify.

careers.employer: Self-employed → Palmer and Hooker law firm
careers.notes: Per Wikipedia + Milwaukee historical record: admitted to bar 1856; same year moved to Milwaukee. Law firm Palmer and Hooker formed 1864 with Henry Palmer. Per period biography: 'a lawyer of note.'
Served three terms as City Attorney per Milwaukee historical record.

21st (some sources say 18th) Mayor of Milwaukee, elected 1872. Population then ~75,000. Mayor-bicameral council form. Predecessor + successor: Harrison Ludington. Major mayoral works: 13 miles of water pipe laid, pumping engines installed, water reservoir built. One-term.

Per Milwaukee historical record + Wikipedia: appointed General Counsel by Henry Palmer (his former law partner, who became NWML president). Held position until sudden death March 1888 from heart disease in Jacksonville FL.

sources.short: Findagrave: David Griswold Hooker (Forest Home Cemetery Milwaukee)
Wisconsin State Bar Association Report of proceedings

narrative_blocks.header: Vermont farm boy to Milwaukee mayor
narrative_blocks.body: David Griswold Hooker — born September 4, 1830 in Poultney VT (named after his mother's Griswold maiden name) — left the family farm for college, graduating Middlebury College in 1853. Admitted to the Wisconsin bar in 1856, he moved to Milwaukee that same year and built his law practice over 16 years. He served as Milwaukee City Attorney three times (first term 1867-1870), formed the law firm of Palmer and Hooker in 1864 with Henry Palmer, and was elected the 21st Mayor of Milwaukee in 1872 — a single one-year term during which he laid 13 miles of water pipe, installed pumping engines, and built the city's reservoir.

narrative_blocks.header: Married twice; lost first wife at 30
narrative_blocks.body: David's first marriage to Sarah Parker Harris of Middlebury VT in 1862 ended with her early death April 27, 1868 at age 30 — likely childbirth complications after the rapid arrival of their three sons (Edward Harris 1863, Frederick Nathaniel 1865, William David 1867). David remarried in 1871 to Julia Almira Ashley of Milwaukee, daughter of Dr. William Bliss Ashley + Julia Cornwall Hall, and rector of Milwaukee's St. Paul's Episcopal Church. Three daughters by Julia: Alice (m.

death.cause: Possibly childbirth complications — third child William David born Oct 1867; she d. April 1868 (6 months later).

burial.plot_notes: Middlebury Cemetery, Middlebury Addison Co VT

burialCemetery.name: Forest Home Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Forest Home Cemetery, Milwaukee WI — oldest continuously-operating cemetery in Milwaukee. Major burial cluster of the David Griswold Hooker (H02448) Mayor of Milwaukee family across 3 generations: David himself + 2nd wife Julia Almira Ashley Hooker (X02310); David's eldest son Edward Harris Hooker (H03908, lawyer, Section 33) + his wife Grace Lawton Hooker + their son Ralph D. Hooker (1895-1922 d.young); David's daughter Alice Ashley McCutcheon (H03911) + husband Frank C. McCutcheon; David's daughters Julia E. Hooker (H03912) + Edith Mary Greenwood (H03913). The Hooker presence at Forest Home spans 1888 (David's mayoral years) through 1975 (Edith's death) — nearly a century.
